title: Many quartic threefolds are not stably rational
speaker: Jean-Louis Colliot-Thélène
tags: Workshop on Chow Groups Motives and Derived Categories

proof_stages.label: Rationality hierarchy and motivating threefold questions
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up the implications rational implies stably rational implies retract rational implies unirational implies rationally connected, then reviews classical cubic and quartic threefold nonrationality results and the remaining stable-rationality problem.

proof_stages.label: Universal CH0-triviality as a stable birational obstruction
proof_stages.summary: Retract rationality is shown to force the degree map on zero-cycles to be an isomorphism over all field extensions, and this is related to decomposition of the diagonal and to the limitations of CH0-triviality over algebraically closed fields.

proof_stages.label: Unramified cohomology beyond the Brauer group
proof_stages.summary: Unramified Galois cohomology is defined through residues on codimension-one points or valuation rings, identified as a stable birational invariant, and compared in low degrees with the Brauer group and the defect of the integral Hodge conjecture.

proof_stages.label: Codimension-two cycle cokernel and cubic tests
proof_stages.summary: A further stable birational invariant built from codimension-two Chow groups after extension to an algebraic closure is introduced and related to H^3_nr, with cubic fourfolds and cubic threefolds used to show both its reach and its limitations.

proof_stages.label: Specialization theorem for universal CH0-triviality
proof_stages.summary: The specialization method is formulated and proved: if a universally CH0-trivial generic fiber specializes to a singular integral fiber whose resolution morphism is universally CH0-trivial, then the resolved special fiber must also be universally CH0-trivial, giving a contrapositive obstruction to stable rationality.

proof_stages.label: Singular quartic degeneration with Artin-Mumford Brauer obstruction
proof_stages.summary: A singular quartic threefold degeneration is constructed from data related to an Artin-Mumford double solid, its resolution is checked to be universally CH0-trivial over the singular fiber, and its nonzero Brauer group supplies the obstruction required by specialization.

proof_stages.label: Many smooth quartic threefolds are not retract rational
proof_stages.summary: By placing the special singular quartic in a one-parameter family or spreading it over number fields and finite fields, the specialization theorem yields smooth quartic hypersurfaces over C, over Qbar, and over function fields that are not retract rational and hence not stably rational.

proof_stages.label: Further applications and nonclosed-field appendix
proof_stages.summary: The final part surveys subsequent specialization results of Beauville and Totaro and then discusses analogous rationality and retract-rationality questions for cubic hypersurfaces over non-algebraically closed fields, ending with open problems.
